palette: Cobalt glass, oxidized brick, and laboratory white
lighting: Overcast spring morning, cool diffused light with clinical interior highlights
instagram_prompts: First-person perspective (FPP) optic sensor capture. Looking out from a high-perched ledge of a Victorian red-brick firehouse. In the background, the sleek, blue-glass geometric towers of a modern biotechnology campus loom over the historic neighborhood. Boston Patriots' Day marathon banners (blue and yellow) flutter in the crisp spring breeze. Palette of oxidized brick and cobalt glass. Cool, diffused morning light. World feels enormous. NO nanobot in frame. No text.

First-person perspective (FPP) optic sensor capture. Inside a sterile, high-tech laboratory. A sequence of glowing micro-fluidic chambers containing translucent cellular cultures. Reflective stainless steel surfaces. Through a glass partition, the warm, out-of-focus glow of a historic library interior. Clinical white lighting with warm amber leak. World is impossibly large. You are the camera, do not render yourself. No text.

First-person perspective (FPP) optic sensor capture. Extreme close-up of a weathered granite cobblestone street in Boston. In the crevices, tiny green spring moss is growing. A discarded, high-tech biometric patch lies partially crushed on the stone. In the background, the blurred motion of runners' legs. Palette of charcoal granite and neon green. Low-angle lighting, long shadows. World is massive. No nanobot. No text.
